data.title: Meitu, Inc. GAAP EPS of ¥0.12, revenue of ¥3.85B
data.description:
• Meitu, Inc. (MEIUF): FY GAAP EPS of ¥0.12.
  • Revenue of ¥3.85B (+28.8% Y/Y).
  • Net profit declined to ¥547.40M in 2025 from ¥806.20M in 2024, indicating a 32.1% YoY decline.
  • Gross profit rose to ¥2,838.80M in 2025 from ¥2,277.80M in 2024, up 24.6% YoY.

data.title: Western Copper and Gold reports FY results
data.description:
• Western Copper and Gold press release (WRN): FY as of December 31, 2025, the company held cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments of C$50.5 million. 
  • As of March 26, 2026, the company held cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments of approximately C$135 million.

data.title: SBC Medical Group GAAP EPS of $0.14 beats by $0.03, revenue of $39.57M misses by $5.87M
data.description:
• SBC Medical Group Holdings press release (SBC): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.14 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $39.57M (-10.9% Y/Y) misses by $5.87M.
  • EBITDA was $14 million, representing a 35% year-over-year decrease. EBITDA margin1 was 34% for the fourth quarter of 2025, representing a year-over-year decrease of 12 percentage points.
  • Return on equity, which is defined as net income attributable to the Company divided by the average shareholder’s equity as of December 31, 2025, was 23% representing a year-over-year increase of 9.8 percentage points.
  • Number of Franchise Locations2 was 283 as of December 31, 2025, representing an increase of 34 locations from December 31, 2024.
  • Number of customers3 in the last twelve months ended December 31, 2025, was 6.6 million, representing a 12% year-over-year increase.
  • Repeat rate for customers4 who visited franchisee’s clinics twice or more was 72%.

data.description: • Seabridge Gold press release (SA): FY GAAP EPS of -$0.53.

data.title: The Las Vegas Strip breaks its gaming win losing streak in February
data.description:
Gaming win on the Las Vegas Strip rose 0.9% in February to $696.3M on a year-over-year comparison. The slight increase broke a streak of three straight months of a year-over-year gaming win decline.

Statewide gaming win was up 1.5% year-over-year in February to $1.24B. Downtown Las Vegas casinos saw gaming win decrease 4.2% during the month to $69.8M. Meanwhile, Reno casinos reported gaming win increased 7.7% during the month to $60.6M.

Across the state, gaming win was up 1.7% for the slot machine business on a win percentage of 7.0%. Table/counter/card game gaming win was up 1.1% on a win percentage of 13.1%. Pai Gow gaming win was up 158%, and baccarat gaming win rose 44% to help offset declines in roulette, 3-card poker, and Ultimate Texas Hold'Em.

On a fiscal year-to-date basis (July 2025-February 2026), Nevada gaming win was up only 0.8%, despite an event calendar seen as favorable for conventions and tourism.

Sector watch: Caesars Entertainment (CZR), MGM Resorts (MGM), Boyd Gaming (BYD), Wynn Resorts (WYNN), Full House Resorts (FLL), Bally's Corporation (BALY), VICI Properties (VICI), Sportradar (SRAD), Genius Sports (GENI), Gaming & Leisure Properties (GLPI), and Red Rock Resorts (RRR).

data.title: Micron steadies after 23% slide as investors weigh Trump's “hottest company” remark
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) shares were little changed in early premarket trading on Friday, following a six-session slide that erased 23% from the stock, as markets weighed President Donald Trump’s “hottest company” remark.

The stock has come under pressure in recent sessions as investors reassess the outlook for memory pricing and AI-driven demand, despite Micron’s (MU) positioning as a key supplier of high-bandwidth memory used in artificial intelligence workloads.

Shares of peers SanDisk (SNDK), Western Digital (WDC) and Seagate (STX) also showed modest moves.

Micron (MU) shares had dropped nearly 7% on Thursday after Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) unveiled its TurboQuant compression technology, which it said reduces memory usage and improves AI model efficiency.

The development raised concerns about potential demand erosion for memory chips, weighing on the broader sector.

Later in the day, Trump said in a Fox News interview that he had recently met with Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra and described the company as one of the “hottest” in the U.S.

data.title: AM Markets Need to Know: Additional troops in Moddle East, oil could go to $200, and more
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher before the bell as U.S. President Donald Trump extended the pause on attacks against Iran’s energy infrastructure into April.

Now, here are five news stories that broke overnight to watch out for:

OpenAI ad revenue hits milestone: OpenAI’s (OPENAI) ad pilot program in the U.S. has generated over $100 million in annualized revenue in just six weeks since launch, according to a company spokesperson. The AI company began showing ads to some users on its free and entry-level paid Go membership tiers. At present, less than 20% of users see the ads, though around 85% are eligible, suggesting significant room to expand monetization efforts.

Pentagon weighs troop deployment: The Defense Department is considering deploying up to 10,000 additional ground troops to the Middle East to expand President Trump’s military options as he weighs potential peace talks with Iran, the Wall Street Journal reported. Trump said Thursday he is pausing strikes on Iran’s energy sector until April 6 to allow negotiations to continue beyond his earlier deadline.

Anthropic wins court order against ban: Anthropic PBC (ANTHRO) secured a preliminary injunction blocking a Trump administration ban on government use of its artificial intelligence technology. U.S. District Judge Rita F. Lin issued the order Thursday, halting the administration’s plan to cut ties with the AI company while the case proceeds in San Francisco federal court. Anthropic argued the move could cost the company billions in lost revenue.

Oil could spike to $200 a barrel: Oil prices could surge as high as $200 a barrel if the Iran conflict extends into June and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, Bloomberg reported, citing an analysis from Macquarie Group. Analysts including Vikas Dwivedi assigned a 40% probability to a scenario where war lasting through the second quarter drives historically high real prices.

Zelenskiy makes Gulf trip: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy made an unannounced visit to the Persian Gulf this week, aiming to trade battlefield expertise in countering drones for additional defense assistance. Arriving in Saudi Arabia, Zelenskiy said he planned to hold key discussions with regional leaders, emphasizing cooperation on security issues.

data.title: Wishpond appoints Jordan Gutierrez as CEO
Meta boosts Texas AI data center spend to $10B
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) has increased the investment for its data center campus in El Paso, Texas, to $10B from $1.5B, the company said in a blog post.

The company is aggressively pouring resources into artificial intelligence to stay ahead of rivals Google (GOOG) (GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN), and Microsoft (MSFT), while scaling back on efforts like the metaverse initiative and making frequent job cuts across the board to offset heavy spending bets.

The Texas data center, which will generate 1 gigawatt of compute power once online by 2028, is expected to support over 4,000 construction jobs during the peak building phase and about 300 operational jobs once it's finished.

The company said it plans to add enough clean and renewable energy to the El Paso grid to match 100% of the electricity use of its data center.

Meta plans to use a water-efficient cooling system for its data centers that will recycle and use the same water, resulting in zero usage for the most part during the year.

"To put that into perspective, our water use is projected to be similar to a typical golf course in West Texas," the company said Thursday.

The company has also pledged to restore 200% of the water consumed by the data center to local watersheds.

In February, Meta began construction of its 1 GW data center in Lebanon, Indiana, the investment for which was also estimated at $10B.

According to data from Meta, it has 31 data centers in the U.S., including those under construction, 127 solar projects, and 62 water facilities.
